特記事項: 【合併】科学技術英語Ａ　４（理学部）/科学技術英語Ａ　４（理工学部）/科学技術英語Ａ　４（工学部）/科学技術英語Ａ　４（生命環境学部）
授業方法: Classes will be conducted face-to-face in the classroom on campus.

第1回:
【Startup Week】Topic：【Introduction to the Course】
"Online class（On-demand format)"／《60 minutes》
Access the following link for details: (insert link here)

Introduction to the course theme, structure, expectations, and assessment. Students are introduced to the concept of science fiction as both cultural product and technological predictor, with an overview of Star Trek (The Original Series and The Next Generation). Key vocabulary related to science fiction, technology, and media is introduced, and students practice discussing familiar science fiction examples in guided speaking activities.
第2回:
【Core Week①】【What Is Science Fiction? Culture, Imagination, and Technology】

"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Students explore definitions and characteristics of science fiction, focusing on how cultural values, hopes, and fears shape imagined futures. Discussion and reading activities focus on genre-specific vocabulary and descriptive language.
第3回:
【Core Week②】【Star Trek: The Original Series – Vision of the Future】

"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Analysis of selected episodes from Star Trek: The Original Series, focusing on its optimistic view of humanity, exploration, and technology. Students examine how language reflects values such as cooperation, ethics, and progress.
第4回:
【Flex Hours】
"Online class（On-demand format)"／《60 minutes》
Viewing / listening / interactive (Google Form or LUNA)
第5回:
【Core Week③】【Technology Inspired by Star Trek】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Students examine real-world technologies inspired by science fiction, such as communicators, tablets, and voice-controlled systems. Emphasis is placed on science and technology vocabulary and cause–effect language.
第6回:
【Flex Hours】
"Online class（On-demand format)"／《60 minutes》
Viewing / listening / interactive (Google Form or LUNA)
第7回:
【Core Week ④】【Artificial Intelligence in Science Fiction】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Introduction to AI through science fiction examples, focusing on characters such as Data (Star Trek: The Next Generation). Students discuss intelligence, consciousness, and ethics using structured discussion tasks.
第8回:
【Core Week ⑤】【Star Trek: The Next Generation – Humanity and Ethics】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Close examination of selected TNG episodes that address moral dilemmas and technological responsibility. Grammar focus includes expressing opinions, agreement, and contrast.
第9回:
【Core Week⑥】【Language of Science and Technology】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Focused vocabulary-building week targeting science, technology, and abstract concepts. Students practice pronunciation and fluency through explanation and paraphrasing activities.
第10回:
【Core Week⑦】【2001: A Space Odyssey – Film, Technology, and Meaning】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Exploration of 2001: A Space Odyssey as a cinematic and philosophical work. Students analyze visual storytelling, minimal dialogue, and themes of AI and human evolution.
第11回:
【Core Week⑧】【Comparing Science Fiction Worlds; Introduction to Presentation】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Comparative analysis of Star Trek and other science fiction works. Students practice comparative structures and advanced descriptive vocabulary. Introduction to presentation and students think about a topic.
Students must submit a presentation topic.
第12回:
【Core Week⑨】【Science Fiction and Real-World Innovation】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Discussion of how science fiction influences scientists, engineers, and designers. Students work with authentic materials and practice summarizing complex ideas.
第13回:
【Core Week⑩】【Ethics, Responsibility, and the Future; Introduction to report】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Students examine ethical questions surrounding AI, automation, and human responsibility, using science fiction scenarios as discussion prompts. Focus on argument structure and clarity. Introduction to report and students think about a topic.
Students must submit a report topic.
第14回:
【Core Week⑪】【Student Presentations and Discussion】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Students present on a selected science fiction work or technology-related theme. Emphasis is placed on fluency, pronunciation, and clear organization.
第15回:
【Core Week⑫】【Student Presentations and Discussion】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Students who have not present in the previous class do so.
第16回:
【Core Week⑬】【Course Review and Reflection; Submission of Report】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Course wrap-up, review of key vocabulary and concepts, and reflection on how science fiction shapes understanding of technology and culture. Students assess their language progress and participation. Students submit their written reports.

成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading1: Participation 20%; Assignments (e.g. presentation, report) 20% x 2= 40%; Homework 25 %; On-demand work 15%

name: 【326】科学技術英語Ａ　４／SciTech English A
担当者: ジェルミナリオ　Ｒ．（GERMINARIO RENATO BRUNO）
